references.title: Predicting student performance using advanced learning analytics
references.journal: 26th International Conference on World Wide Web Companion

extracted.Features: [Electricity Bill, Natural Gas Bill, Telephonne Bill, Water Bill, Food Expenses, Miscellaneous Expenditure, Medical, Family Expenditure on Education, Accomodation Expenses, Studying Family Members, Dependent Family Member, Father Income, Mother Income, Land Income, Miscellaneous Income, Earning Hands, Father Status, Father Retired, Guardian Alive, Gender, Marital Statuys, House Ownership, Previous Program Scholarship, Previous Institution Type, Self-Employed, Land Value, Bank Balance, Stock Value, House Value, House Condition, Miscellaneous Assest Value, Location, Number of Vehicles]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Initial Dataset comprised about 3000 students but was subsequently cut to 776 instances.

Later, due to class imbalance (only 86 students had faild to complete degree) authors decide to create their models on a subset of 100 students (50/50).

Authors develop their own sets of features and test their effectiveness.
extracted.Models:
C4.5
CART
Bayes Networks
Naive Bayes
SVM
extracted.Comments:
Experiments are done 2-fold:

1. Analysis of impact of individual Predictors, 

2. Performance of the Feature Set with Different Classifiers.

10 of the features created were not used in the models later due to being irrelevant/redundant.
extracted.Limitations:
Small Dataset

Feature Set is not feasible to come by since it requires multiple sets of personally identifiable information.

annotations.text: EDM and LA are useful disciplines to extract insights from educational databases. Most of themethods that predict student performance use income or assets, but often neglect spending or personal information.

The authors use data from Pakistani universities to create a model that predicts success on a the degree using these often overlooked features. The approach outperforms outperforms other models that do not consider these new feature sets.
